ships.name: 100i
ships.description: The Origin 100i is a luxury starter ship that is the base variant of the 100 series. It features Origin Jumpworks' patented AIR Fuel System, making it the most efficient and eco-friendly ship on the market. It is capable of long-distance flights compared to most ships of its size.[1][2]

ships.name: 125a
ships.description: The Origin 125a is a light fighter and starter ship that is the combat variant of the 100 series. With the AIR Fuel System, a souped-up weapons package, and all the luxury and refinement you've come to expect from Origin Jumpworks, the 125a has been designed for the discerning maverick.[1]

ships.name: 135c
ships.description: The Origin 135c is a light freight and starter ship that is the cargo variant of the 100 series. The ship has a bespoke cargo module integrated in the underside rear of the ship to accommodate the additional capacity along with Origin's patented AIR fuel system.[1][2]

ships.name: 300i
ships.description: The 300i serves as the foundational model within a series of luxury touring spacecraft created by Origin Jumpworks. This model is characterized by its premium features and aesthetic design, appealing to a wealthier clientele that values a visually appealing spacecraft over a more functional, utilitarian option.[1]

ships.name: 315p
ships.description: The 315p represents a variant of the 300 touring spacecraft, which was created by Origin Jumpworks. It features upgraded engines that provide superior power performance, along with a custom communications system developed by Chimera Communications. Typically, the 315p serves as a support vessel for larger exploration teams or is utilized by individual owners seeking to embark on journeys in a spacecraft that embodies Origin's distinctive aesthetic.[1]

ships.name: 350r
ships.description: The 350r represents a high-performance racing model within the 300 series of spacecraft created by Origin Jumpworks. As the fastest variant in the series, the 350r channels the entire power output of the 300 into achieving unparalleled speed, all while preserving the unique design characteristics associated with Origin.[1]

ships.name: 400i
ships.description: The 400i is a high-performance luxury pathfinder manufactured by Origin Jumpworks. With greater range compared to other ships in its class, advanced exploration equipment, and substantial defensive capabilities, the 400i is a ship that can explore the most unforgiving sectors of the 'verse while retaining the comfort and elegance that Origin is well known for.[1]

ships.name: 600i Executive Edition
ships.description: The limited 600i Executive Edition is a multi-role luxury exploration vessel produced by Origin Jumpworks. They partnered with leading designers and influencers from the Chairman’s Club to elevate the already impressive 600i, resulting in an 'Executive Edition' that features a refined finish and stunning gold accents. This exceptional creation is exclusively available to the most esteemed members of the Chairman’s Club, known as the 'Legatus Navium', reflecting the high standards of its originators.

ships.name: 600i Explorer
ships.description: The 600i Explorer, a versatile luxury craft from Origin Jumpworks, showcases a meticulously crafted hull that harmonizes performance with adaptability, all presented in an elegant and enduring design. This vessel incorporates advanced modular technology, enabling users to tailor the ship to their specific requirements. If your ambition is to make a mark in history by uncovering a new star system, the Explorer module of the 600i replaces the lounge with a sophisticated scanning station and adds extra utility hardpoints, thereby enhancing the ship's operational capabilities.[1]

ships.name: 600i Touring
ships.description: The 600i Touring from Origin Jumpworks is a luxury multi-role spacecraft which showcases an intricately crafted hull that harmonizes performance and adaptability within an elegant and enduring aesthetic. The 600i incorporates advanced modular technology, enabling you to tailor the vessel to suit your specific requirements. The Touring module provides a comfortable environment for your guests, featuring exquisite furnishings from some of the Empire's leading designers.[1]

ships.name: 85X
ships.description: The Origin 85X is a well-designed and carefully crafted 2-person spacecraft, offering a wide range of capabilities for travel both within and outside of planetary atmospheres. With its advanced thruster technology, which is similar to that of the 300 series, this vessel combines the speed of a racing ship with the dependability of a touring vessel. Whether you're exploring the surface of a planet or simply enjoying the views of your star system, this sleek spacecraft upholds Origin's longstanding reputation for capturing attention.[1][2]

ships.name: 890 Jump
ships.description: The Origin 890 Jump is an interstellar super-yacht designed to immerse its passengers in opulent luxury as the ship cruises from system to system.[1] Some 890 Jumps are privately owned by extremely wealthy individuals, in which case they serve as the ultimate social hotspots for friends, family, and guests. Other 890s are commercial vessels that focus on the elite tourism industry.[2]

ships.name: A1 Spirit
ships.description: The A1 Spirit represents a mid-sized, multi-functional light bomber produced by Crusader Industries. This company extends its renowned dedication to quality into the civilian sector with the A1 Spirit, a versatile starship designed primarily as a light bomber while possessing the adaptability to perform a variety of tasks and respond to unforeseen challenges.[1][2]

ships.name: A2 Hercules Starlifter
ships.description: The A2 Hercules Starlifter is a military heavy gunship and bomber developed by Crusader Industries. The A2 gunship has been used to devastating effect in airborne assaults, search and rescue operations, and landing initiatives. With more than double the firepower of the M2, and a custom bomb bay capable of delivering a staggering payload, the A2 caters to anyone hauling massive amounts of cargo through potentially unfriendly skies.[1]

ships.name: ATLS
ships.description: The ATLS (Assisted Transport and Loading System)[1] is a power suit manufactured by Argo Astronautics. It is designed to assist individuals in lifting heavy loads when manual handling poses safety risks and the use of a vehicle is not feasible. This durable exo-frame incorporates a patented structural support mechanism along with a custom tractor system, which greatly enhances the user's strength, stability, and load-bearing capabilities.[2]

ships.name: ATLS GEO
ships.description: The ATLS GEO is mining variant of the ATLS power suit developed by Argo Astronautics. Using the robust framework provided by the ATLS, the engineers at Argo have skillfully transformed the power suit into an optimal personal mining platform. Equipped with a mining laser, tractor beam, and a 0.1 SCU detachable ore storage, the ATLS GEO serves as a comprehensive solution for maximizing the value extracted from your deposits.[1]

ships.name: ATLS IKTI

ships.name: Apollo Medivac
ships.description: The RSI Apollo Medivac is a hardened variant of the Apollo focusing on combat search and rescue with enhanced missile racks and additional armor.[1] The 2948 Medivac additionally pays homage to the classic 2910 film, Astromedics: Back from the Brink, with livery that accurately recreates the headlining Kithara.[2]

items.label: RSI Apollo Medivac S02 Missile Rack

ships.name: Apollo Triage
ships.description: The Apollo Triage is an armored medical aerospace-craft manufactured by Roberts Space Industries for use when a ground-based medical facility is not available.[1] Combining the ambulance capabilities of the Drake Cutlass Red with the advanced medical technology found in much larger facilities, the Apollo is the gold standard in medical evacuation and rapid emergency care, having provided critical aid to the known universe for over two centuries. When one thinks of first-class medical rescue, one thinks of the RSI Apollo.[1][2]

ships.name: Ares Star Fighter Inferno
ships.description: The Ares Star Fighter Inferno, produced by Crusader Industries, is an anti-capital fighter equipped with a Behring manufactured Size 7 ballistic gatling. Its sustained fire capability is devastating, overpowering adversaries with a relentless barrage that can take down smaller vessels and rapidly shred the hulls of larger ones. While formidable on its own, the Inferno is an effective complement to the Ares Ion in capital ship engagements, as its armor-piercing rounds can be paired with the Ion's shield-disabling shots. This combination enhances the Inferno's effectiveness in hunting down capital ships.[1]

ships.name: Ares Star Fighter Ion
ships.description: The Ares Star Fighter Ion is an anti-capital fighter manufactured by Crusader Industries. Designed to lead the charge against capital ships and heavily armed flotillas, the Ares ION excels at spearheading a fleet of fighters and support ships. Its purpose-built Behring SF7E laser cannon accurately delivers high-powered shots to quickly disable the shields of gunships and, with the support of other Ares variants, efficiently incapacitate capital-class vessels.[1]

ships.name: Arrastra
ships.description: The Arrastra, a mining platform produced by Roberts Space Industries (RSI), is designed to address the numerous hazards associated with mining the abundant and valuable resources found throughout the universe. With powerful remote-controlled laser heads, a shifting top/bottom mining turret, space for ground mining vehicles, an onboard refinery, and a comfortable living area, the Arrastra is perfectly suited to long-haul multi-crew mining operations.[1][2]

ships.name: Arrow
ships.description: The Arrow is a light fighter developed by Anvil Aerospace. It distinguishes itself with its unparalleled design, exceptional top speed, and highly responsive maneuverability, establishing it as the most agile scrapper within its category. Equipped with a formidable array of weaponry that ensures ample firepower, this aircraft epitomizes the quintessential light fighter, re-imagined for the forthcoming era of warfare.[1]

ships.name: Asgard
ships.description: The Asgard is a variant of the Valkyrie series of transport ships developed by Anvil Aerospace. Designed to be the next evolution in ground vehicle transport, the Asgard represents a significant advancement that is tailored to meet the requirements of contemporary military operations. It features a rapid-loading ramp, a roomy interior specifically engineered to safely house your chosen assault vehicle, and a comprehensive array of weapons to ensure entry into the most fiercely contested drop zones.[1][2]

ships.name: Aurora Mk I CL
ships.description: The RSI Aurora CL (also known as the Clipper) is a member of the Aurora MK I series lineup. A ship designed for mercantile and trading excursions, the Aurora Clipper is the perfect vessel for aspiring entrepreneurs and seasoned traders alike. Swapping a smaller power plant and armor capabilities for an expanded cargo capacity, the Clipper ups the ante for personal merchant craft.[1]

items.label: Aurora Mk I CL Ship Armor

ships.name: Aurora Mk I LN
ships.description: The RSI Aurora LN (also known as the Legionnaire) is a combat variant of the Aurora MK I series lineup developed with assistance from Anvil Aerospace. It features a more robust shield generator and a pair of additional weapon hardpoints.[1]

ships.name: Aurora Mk I LX
ships.description: The RSI Aurora LX (also known as the Deluxe) is the luxury variant in the Aurora MK I series. The Deluxe features patent leather interior to guarantee comfort for those long stretches in the deep black.[1]

items.label: Aurora Mk I LX Ship Armor

ships.name: Aurora Mk I MR
ships.description: The RSI Aurora MR (also known as the Marque) is a spaceship focused on versatility. Part of the Aurora MK I series lineup, the Marque comes with a pair of Behring lasers and a high quality gun cooler system.[1]

items.label: Aurora Mk I MR Ship Armor

ships.name: Aurora Mk I SE
ships.description: The RSI Aurora SE (also known as the Special Edition) is the final variant in the Aurora MK I series. The Special Edition serves as a send-off to the line-up, combining the traits of all previous variants, the versatile nature of the Marque, the cargo hauling from the Clipper, the combat prowess of the Legionnaire, and the premium design of the Deluxe.[1][2][3]

ships.name: Aurora Mk II
ships.description: The Aurora Mk II is a small starter multi-role ship, manufactured by Roberts Space Industries. The designers have reinterpreted the classic ship with a fresh perspective, completely redesigning the reliable Aurora from the ground up. This new version enhances performance, usability, and aesthetics for a new generation of pilots. It also features interchangeable modules, allowing for easy specialization without limiting career paths. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced pilot looking for an upgrade, the Aurora Mk II is ready for you.[1][2][3]
