ships.name: Avenger Stalker
ships.description: The Aegis Stalker was the first developed variant of the Avenger carrier-based fighter, an interdiction variant intended for police use and popular with bounty hunters.[1] Heir to the Avenger's long and storied career as the standard patrol craft of the UEE Advocacy and UEE Navy service before that, the Stalker features a sturdy and reliable hull, larger-than-expected engine mounts, and the Stalker Prisoner Transport Module.[1][2]

ships.name: Avenger Titan
ships.description: The Aegis Titan is a leading variant of the Avenger fighter series redesigned with a dedicated cargo bay that replaces much of the original carrier-based fighter's rear cabin.[1] With the extra cargo space and the Avenger's tried and true combat abilities, the Titan is a light cargo hauler that's more than capable of handling itself in a fight.[2]

ships.name: Avenger Titan Renegade
ships.description: The Avenger Titan Renegade is a special edition variant of the Avenger Titan manufactured by Aegis Dynamics. Developed as part of the Masters of Flight series in, collaboration with the flight simulation Arena Commander, the Renegade serves as a homage to the renowned pilot Danny Solomon, recognizing his contributions to the Advocacy in the pursuit of justice in Bremen. This variant of the Avenger is outfitted with a tailored loadout specifically designed for dogfighting, along with a unique special edition livery that pays tribute to this legendary vessel.[1]

ships.name: Avenger Warlock
ships.description: The Aegis Warlock is a variant of the Avenger carrier-based fighter in active (albeit not exclusive) service with the Advocacy and the Navy, intended for suspect apprehension and battlefield support missions.[1] Outfitted with a Behring REP-8 EMP Generator contained within the original Avenger's rear cabin, the Warlock is capable of emitting a powerful electromagnetic wave to disable any electronics unfortunate enough to be within the blast radius.[2]

ships.name: Ballista
ships.description: The Ballista is a ground-based anti-aircraft vehicle hailing from the Atlas Platform developed by Anvil Aerospace. This mobile missile defense system is specifically engineered to protect ground locations against aerial threats. It boasts an impressive array of heavy missiles and a quiet signature, allowing the Ballista to proficiently address danger from the skies with a deadly surprise.[1][2]

ships.name: Ballista Dunestalker
ships.description: The Ballista Dunestalker is a limited edition, specialized ground vehicle manufactured by Anvil Aerospace. It is designed for anti-air operations, and is equipped with ground-to-air torpedoes. It boasts eight size 5 missiles and two size 7 torpedoes, complemented by a pair of gatling guns mounted on a rotatable turret. This vehicle serves as a dedicated anti-air defense system, effectively deterring aerial threats. The Ballista, similar to the Spartan and the Centurion, was developed from a shared foundational platform, the Atlas series.[1]

ships.name: Ballista Snowblind
ships.description: The Ballista Snowblind is a limited edition, specialized ground vehicle manufactured by Anvil Aerospace. It is designed for anti-air operations, and is equipped with ground-to-air torpedoes. It boasts eight size 5 missiles and two size 7 torpedoes, complemented by a pair of gatling guns mounted on a rotatable turret. This vehicle serves as a dedicated anti-air defense system, effectively deterring aerial threats. The Ballista, similar to the Spartan and the Centurion, was developed from a shared foundational platform, the Atlas series.[1]

ships.name: Blade (replica)
ships.description: The Esperia Blade is a Vanduul light fighter replica produced by Esperia for military use. They are often used for training for the UEE pilots.[1] The maneuverability of the Blade is beyond anything else out there which makes up for its lacking armor.[2][3]

ships.name: Buccaneer
ships.description: The Buccaneer is a light fighter developed by Drake Interplanetary. It has been engineered to operate and engage in combat in a manner that reflects the lifestyle of its users. This agile craft is built to navigate and contend effectively beyond its expected capabilities, and complements fellow Drake ships like the Cutlass Black or the Caterpillar. As a resilient frontier combat vessel, it is capable of being serviced under the most challenging conditions, ensuring the survival of genuine, operational space crews.[1]

ships.name: C1 Spirit
ships.description: The C1 Spirit is a medium-size hauler developed by Crusader Industries. It serves as an essential logistical asset for any operation, facilitating the transportation of critical supplies and the delivery of ground exploration vehicles. Its spacious cargo capacity, combined with the renowned Crusader design, positions it as the optimal choice for pilots aiming to transport substantial loads while maintaining ease of use. Featuring an ample cargo hold and a rail-mounted tractor beam, the C1 Spirit serves as a versatile and adaptable vessel for Citizens navigating the 'verse on a regular basis.[1][2][3]

ships.name: C2 Hercules Starlifter
ships.description: The C2 Hercules Starlifter is a civilian heavy lift transport developed by Crusader Industries. Utilizing the patented Hercules military-grade spaceframe and expanding cargo capacity, while sacrificing barely any firepower, the C2 has taken the private sector by storm. It has become the industry standard for racing teams, ship dealers and manufacturers, construction orgs, mining corporations, and even large-scale touring entertainment outfits.[1]

ships.name: C8R Pisces Rescue
ships.description: The C8R Pisces Rescue is a small medical ship manufactured by Anvil Aerospace, often deployed from a Carrack pathfinder or other Large ship. It is the medical variant of the C8 Pisces that is a deft and swift search and rescue ship. An onboard Tier 3 Medical bed allows the Pisces to treat minor injuries better than a MedPen; stabilizing patients on their way to a larger medical facility.[1]

ships.name: C8X Pisces Expedition
ships.description: The Anvil C8X Pisces Expedition is a special variant of the C8 Pisces. It can be stored inside the Carrack and can be launched from its hangar bay. The ship is meant for scouting, reconnaissance and exploring environments inaccessible to the Carrack. It also features two additional jump seats for extra passengers. It also contains space for 4 SCU of cargo.[1] Additionally, while it doesn't come equipped with one standard, the ship can also be equipped with a Jump Module.[2] The C8X variant is recognizable by its slightly whiter paint job than the standard C8 Pisces, and it has four weapon hardpoints instead of two.[3] During the 2949 Intergalactic Aerospace Expo it was made available on the pledge store for US$45.

ships.name: CSV-SM
ships.description: The CSV-SM (Construction Support Vehicle - Supply Model) is a freight variant of the CSV series of ground vehicles developed by Argo Astronautics. It is specifically designed for the transportation of cargo from one location to another, proving especially effective for the transfer of goods among various outposts and particularly for the transportation of construction materials.[1][2][3]

ships.name: Carrack
ships.description: The Anvil Carrack is a multi-crew explorer that has more than earned its long-standing status as the go-to pathfinder for both military and civilian services. The Carrack comes with two additional craft - Anvil's own specially designed Pisces snub, and an RSI Ursa Rover.[1] It is built as a single, self-sustaining ship that can make long-duration voyages through the roughest areas of space. It's designed specifically for transiting jump points, dealing with extreme conditions in space and getting back home.[2]

ships.name: Carrack Expedition
ships.description: The Carrack Expedition is a special edition variant of the Carrack which is a large multi-crew explorer vessel developed by Anvil Aerospace. It was created as part of the 2950 Carrack launch and showcases a distinctive limited-edition livery that celebrates the fearless crews piloting Carracks bravely into the unknown. Engineered to withstand the harshest environments in both space and atmospheric conditions, this self-sufficient explorer has rightfully secured its reputation as the preferred choice for both military and civilian operations.[1]

ships.name: Caterpillar
ships.description: The Caterpillar is a large cargo ship manufactured by Drake Interplanetary. It has established itself as a dependable and economically viable multi-role vessel, suitable for a wide range of applications, including commercial activities and combat assistance. Recognized as a robust alternative to the widely used Hull series, the Caterpillar is a freighter that offers substantial armament and extensive customization options.[1][2]

ships.name: Caterpillar Best In Show Edition
ships.description: The Caterpillar Best In Show Edition is a distinctive variant of the Caterpillar large cargo ship, manufactured by Drake Interplanetary. This special edition was created especially for the 'Best In Show' event at the Intergalactic Aerospace Expo 2949 and has a unique white and purple livery. The Caterpillar has gained a reputation as a reliable and cost-effective multi-role vessel, adept for various uses, including commercial operations and combat support. It is acknowledged as a strong alternative to the popular Hull series, providing significant firepower and a wide array of customization possibilities.[1][2]

ships.name: Caterpillar Pirate Edition
ships.description: The Caterpillar Pirate Edition is a distinctive variant of the Caterpillar large cargo ship, manufactured by Drake Interplanetary. This special edition showcases a unique color scheme that embodies the thematic aspects of piracy. The Caterpillar has gained a reputation as a reliable and cost-effective multi-role vessel, adept for various uses, including commercial operations and combat support. It is acknowledged as a strong alternative to the popular Hull series, providing significant firepower and a wide array of customization possibilities.[1][2]

ships.name: Centurion
ships.description: The Anvil Centurion is a ground vehicle specializing in short-range anti-air weaponry, employing a powerful quad-turret of energy cannons to bring down any airborne threat. It is built on the same Atlas Platform, like the Ballista and the Spartan.[1][2]

ships.name: Clipper
ships.description: The Clipper is a multi-functional ship developed by Drake Interplanetary. This vessel is engineered for versatility and multiple functions. Equipped with a cargo bay, medical facilities, and crafting capabilities, the Clipper serves as an ideal all-rounder for any emerging spacefaring venture.[1][2][3]

ships.name: Constellation Andromeda
ships.description: The Constellation Andromeda is a multi-person freighter manufactured by Roberts Space Industries (RSI). It is the base model of the Constellation series. It comes with a P-52 Merlin docked at the stern as a snub fighter.[1]

ships.name: Constellation Aquila
ships.description: The Constellation Aquila is a multi-person spacecraft manufactured by Roberts Space Industries (RSI). It is the expedition variant of the Constellation series. It comes with a P-52 Merlin docked at the stern as a snub fighter, and an Ursa parked in the cargo bay as a ground rover. Compared to the base model, the Aquila features a cockpit with improved visibility and an advanced sensor suite in place of the top turret.[1]

ships.name: Constellation Phoenix
ships.description: The Constellation Phoenix is a multi-crew luxury transport spacecraft developed by Roberts Space Industries. As the premium model of the Constellation series, this version is expertly designed to cater to the needs of luxury travel and VIP transport, boasting a range of opulent features. It includes a hidden compartment that is sensor-dampened, specifically intended for securing your most prized possessions. Furthermore, the Phoenix comes with an RSI Lynx rover and a Kruger P-72 Archimedes fighter.[1][2]

ships.name: Constellation Phoenix Emerald
ships.description: The Constellation Phoenix Emerald is a special edition multi-crew luxury spacecraft manufactured by Roberts Space Industries. This variant was specifically designed to commemorate the widely celebrated Stella Fortuna holiday and features a unique dark green and ivory color scheme. As the premium model within the Constellation series, this variant is specifically designed to cater to high-end tourism & VIP transportation needs and boasts a range of luxurious amenities.[1][2]

ships.name: Constellation Taurus
ships.description: The Constellation Taurus is the freighter variant of the large multi-crew Constellation series manufactured by Roberts Space Industries (RSI). It trades the side missile racks and the snub fighter for additional cargo space, a cargo release system for quick unloading, and a security hold reinforced with tech plating.[1][2]

ships.name: Corsair
ships.description: The Drake Corsair is a multi-crew explorer ship that gives up defenses for a focus on versatility and firepower. It has asymmetric convertible wings, and a large cargo hold. Up to four crew can be accommodated in the Corsair's functional quarters, and a full complement of scanning suites along with a pair of quantum fuel tanks provide the means and method of exploring distant, dangerous stars.[1]

ships.name: Crucible
ships.description: The Anvil Crucible (commonly referred as the flying toolbox) is the company's first dedicated repair ship. Featuring a rotating control bridge and a detachable pressurized workspace, the Crucible is a versatile mobile garage equipped with repair arms, a drone operation center and all the equipment needed to overhaul a damaged craft back into fighting shape.[1]

ships.name: Cutlass Black
ships.description: The Drake AS-1 Cutlass Black (also: DCB1) is a low-cost, easy-to-maintain solution for local in-system militia units. The larger-than-average cargo hold, Radar intercept officer (RIO) back-seat and dedicated tractor mount are, the company literature insists, for facilitating search and rescue operations.[1] The Cutlass Black is very popular among pirates.

ships.name: Cutlass Black Best In Show Edition
ships.description: The Cutlass Black Best In Show Edition is a distinctive variant of the Cutlass medium-sized, multi-functional spacecraft manufactured by Drake Interplanetary. This special edition was created especially for the 'Best In Show' event at the Intergalactic Aerospace Expo 2949 and has a unique white and purple livery. Drake asserts that it offers a cost-effective and low-maintenance option for local in-system militia units. The company literature emphasizes that the above-average cargo capacity, RIO seat, and specialized tractor mount are designed to enhance search and rescue missions. [1]

ships.name: Cutlass Blue
ships.description: The Drake AS-1 Cutlass Blue (also known as DCB2) is the go-to patrol ship for militia and law enforcement. Featuring an on-board Quantum dampener, a versatile weapons package, and a bank of prisoner containment systems, the Cutlass Blue is built to protect citizens and suppress dangerous outlaws.[1][2]

ships.name: Cutlass Red
ships.description: The Drake Cutlass Red (also known as DCR2) is an emergency response and support ship with a comprehensive medical bay, long-range radar capabilities, and a suite of defensive options. More than just a top-flight emergency responder, the Cutlass Red is a highly regarded support vessel for hazardous combat, recon, and exploration. With the ability to revive fallen squadmates and a full on-board AutoDoc, a single visit to the med bay will ensure that you will always be pulled back from the brink of death.[1]

ships.name: Cutlass Steel
ships.description: The Drake Cutlass Steel is a troop transport model in the Cutlass series with an additional remote turret, more missiles, and 5 door-mounted Gatling guns. With 18 jump seats for armed and armored passengers,[1] the Cutlass Steel is designed to rapidly insert troops into an area while providing fire support for them.

ships.name: Cutter
ships.description: The Cutter is a single-seat starter ship manufactured by Drake Interplanetary . It is a compact and robust spacecraft that caters to individuals from all walks of life. It boasts a cargo capacity of 4 SCU and is equipped with ample power to tackle any challenge. The Cutter is designed to cater to hard-working individuals who require a reliable and efficient spacecraft to accomplish their tasks. With its rugged and versatile design, the Cutter is an ideal choice for anyone seeking a spacecraft that can handle any situation. It embodies the essence of Drake, which is to empower individuals to reach for the stars, regardless of their background or profession.[1][2]

ships.name: Cutter Rambler
ships.description: The Cutter Rambler, a variant for the Cutter, is produced by Drake Interplanetary. Designed with durability, adaptability, and ease of use as its core principles, the Rambler is an ideal vessel for aspiring pilots seeking their place in our vast universe. The ship is equipped with essential features such as an additional fuel tank, a food maker, storage for armor, and ample cargo space, making it an excellent choice for extended missions and adventurous journeys.

ships.name: Cutter Scout
ships.description: The Cutter Scout, produced by Drake Interplanetary, is a solitary spacecraft designed to accommodate a single occupant. Expanding upon the versatile nature of its predecessor, the original Cutter, the Scout enhances its combat and utility capabilities through the incorporation of upgraded weaponry and scanning components. Whether providing assistance to a limited fleet or embarking on independent missions, the Cutter Scout serves as a flexible introductory vessel that maintains its essential qualities without making any compromises.

ships.name: Cyclone
ships.description: The Tumbril Cyclone is an all-terrain vehicle, perfect for land-based operations that require speed and discretion. The baseline model of the Cyclone features an open flatbed in the back where cargo pieces can be safely secured and transported, making it a perfect transport for homesteads or as a short-range vehicle for planetary deliveries[1]

ships.name: Cyclone AA
ships.description: The Cyclone AA is a specialized variant of the Cyclone series of ground vehicles, designed specifically for civilian anti-aircraft operations. This model has been developed by Tumbril Land Systems, a company known for its innovative contributions to ground vehicle technology. The primary function of the Cyclone AA is to provide robust protection for ground forces against various aerial threats, thereby enhancing the safety and operational effectiveness of military personnel on the ground.[1]

ships.name: Cyclone MT
ships.description: The Cyclone MT is a specialized variant of the Cyclone series of ground vehicles, designed specifically to be against air and ground targets. This model has been developed by Tumbril Land Systems, a company known for its innovative contributions to ground vehicle technology. In response to the positive feedback received concerning their popular tactical vehicle line, Tumbril has taken significant steps to expand their product offerings which seeks to meet the evolving demands of military and tactical applications, ensuring that their vehicles are not only well-received but also effective in real-world scenarios.[1][2]

ships.name: Cyclone RC
ships.description: The Cyclone RC is a specialized variant of the Cyclone series of ground vehicles, designed specifically for racing. This model has been developed by Tumbril Land Systems, a company known for its innovative contributions to ground vehicle technology. It incorporates an enhanced intake system designed to facilitate regulated speed bursts, along with features that enable handling customization.[1]

ships.name: Cyclone RN
ships.description: The Cyclone RN is a specialized variant of the Cyclone series of ground vehicles, designed specifically for civilian reconnaissance. This model has been developed by Tumbril Land Systems, a company known for its innovative contributions to ground vehicle technology. The Cyclone RN enhances mobility and situational awareness, making it an ideal light reconnaissance vehicle for scouting missions. It offers comprehensive and precise terrain scans, along with the capability for beacon placement.[1][2]

ships.name: Cyclone TR
ships.description: The Cyclone TR is a specialized variant of the Cyclone series of ground vehicles, designed specifically to be against ground targets. This model has been developed by Tumbril Land Systems, a company known for its innovative contributions to ground vehicle technology. It is equipped with enhanced armor and a manned operated gun turret that offers a comprehensive range of ground fire, making it well-suited for militia and security operations.[1]

ships.name: Defender
ships.description: The Banu Defender ( Banu: oktæ banu@font-face{font-family:"TemplateStyles-Banu-Regular";src:local("Banu"),local("Banu Regular"),local("Banu-Regular"),url("https://starcitizen.tools/resources/fonts/Banu-Regular.woff2")format("woff2"),url("https://starcitizen.tools/resources/fonts/Banu-Regular.woff")format("woff");font-weight:normal;font-style:normal}.mw-parser-output .lang-ochoa{font-family:"TemplateStyles-Banu-Regular";font-feature-settings:"liga"1,"calt"1,"dlig"1,"salt"1}(oktæ Banu).mw-parser-output ruby{line-height:1.25}.mw-parser-output ruby>rt{font-size:0.65rem}; ).mw-parser-output .lang__label{font-size:0.875rem} is a small two-seat combat ship utilized by the Banu to provide protection and fly alongside the Merchantman. Its design features Xi'an thrusters, Tevarin shields, and four Singe tachyon cannons. As a result, with a light-weight hull, it allows the ship to be agile and fast. Though cargo space is limited, the Defender features modest accommodations for its crew and provides easy access to components.[1][2]

ships.name: Dragonfly Black
ships.description: The Dragonfly Black is an open canopy snub ship developed by Drake Interplanetary. It serves as an ideal vehicle for those who seek thrilling experiences. With no barriers between the pilot and the perils of outer space, the Dragonfly embodies both adventure and functionality. Its dual-mode conversion enables operation both on terrestrial surfaces and in the vacuum of space, while a rear-facing second seat accommodates a passenger, enhancing the overall experience.[1]

ships.name: Dragonfly Star Kitten
ships.description: The Dragonfly 'Star Kitten', produced by Drake Interplanetary, is a limited edition open-canopy vehicle engineered for functionality in both terrestrial environments and outer space. Its design is inspired by Sally, the cherished protagonist of the animated series Star Kitten, and was launched to commemorate the fifteenth anniversary of her character.[1][2]

ships.name: Dragonfly Yellowjacket
ships.description: The limited edition Dragonfly 'Yellowjacket' represents a personal open-canopy vehicle designed for both terrestrial and aerial operations, manufactured by Drake Interplanetary. Engineered for compatibility with larger spacecraft, it complements Drake's fleet, which includes models such as the Cutlass and Caterpillar. The Dragonfly serves dual purposes, functioning as a ground transport and as a snub craft that assists in various space missions. However, it lacks the capability for planetary reentry.[1]

ships.name: E1 Spirit
ships.description: The E1 Spirit represents a medium-sized VIP transport vessel engineered by Crusader Industries. This ship is equipped with luxurious cabins, each offering stunning external vistas. While passenger comfort is a primary concern, their safety is equally prioritized through an array of armaments and countermeasures, in addition to an escape raft, enabling travelers to explore the marvels of the universe with peace of mind.[1][2]

ships.name: Eclipse
ships.description: The Aegis Eclipse is a mid-range stealth bomber designed for tactical torpedo strikes on installations or large ships. Concealed engines and internally-stored weapons allow the Eclipse to stealthily deliver its payload with a minimum of risk of counter-attack. After extensive service with the UEE, this high-tech military equipment is making its debut on the civilian market for 2947.[1]

ships.name: Endeavor
ships.description: The MISC Endeavor-class research vessel is a fully modular space platform designed to be adapted for a variety of scientific and medical tasks. Initially developed as a floating laboratory, the MISC Endeavor can be outfitted for everything from spatial telescopy to use as mobile hospital.[1]

ships.name: Expanse
ships.description: The Expanse is a single-seat refinery ship developed by Musashi Industrial and Starflight Concern. It is designed to process front-line raw ore and then either transfer to a dedicated cargo ship or sell the refined ore itself. Ideal in combination with mining vehicles such as the Prospector and/or cargo ships like the Hull A, the Expanse shines as a critical part of an organized mining team.[1][2][3]

ships.name: F7A Hornet Mk II
ships.description: The Anvil F7A Hornet Mk II, introduced in 2944, is the United Empire of Earth Navy's current baseline space superiority fighter; heir to a century-and-longer tradition forged by the original F7A Hornet Mk I. Hornets are primarily deployed on carriers and are used for a variety of missions including combat air patrol, interception, ground attack, reconnaissance in force, escort and strike operations.[1][2]

items.label: Anvil Hornet Mk II S3 Nose Turret
C5-173f S5 Hornet Mk II Ball Turret

ships.name: F7C Hornet Mk I
ships.description: Ceasing central manufacture in 2954, the F7C Hornet Mk I is a newfound classic and the later-developed civilian version of the original F7A Hornet built by Anvil Aerospace. To adversaries, it represented a formidable weapon that should never be taken lightly. For allies, it served as a protector. Those Mk I Hornets still active in the 'Verse remain the reliable and robust multi-role fighter that still echoes the fighters and pilots of the UEE Navy.

ships.name: F7C Hornet Mk II
ships.description: The F7C Hornet Mk II is a civilian version of the F7A Hornet Mk II space-superiority fighter developed by Anvil Aerospace. The re-engineered Hornet, designed with the latest enhancements from the front lines, is now accessible to private pilots throughout the empire. This fighter aircraft boasts an upgraded power system, complemented by a revamped arsenal of weapons and missiles.[1][2]

ships.name: F7C Hornet Wildfire Mk I
ships.description: The F7C Hornet Wildfire Mk I is a special edition variant of the F7C Hornet Mk I developed by Anvil Aerospace, and was created as part of the Masters of Flight series in partnership with the flight simulation Arena Commander. The Wildfire serves as a homage to the esteemed pilot Aria Reilly, recognizing her significant contributions to the renowned Squadron 42.[1]

ships.name: F7C-M Hornet Heartseeker Mk II
ships.description: The F7C-M Hornet Heartseeker Mk II is an aftermarket upgrade kit for the F7 Hornet Mk II series of ships, developed by Anvil Aerospace. Tailored for aviators who are passionate about the exhilarating experience of intense aerial combat, the limited-edition F7C-M Heartseeker Mk II boasts a formidable weaponry system. This iteration is poised to capture the admiration of a new generation of dedicated combat enthusiasts.[1]

items.label: Anvil Hornet Mk II S3 Nose Turret

ships.name: F7C-M Super Hornet Heartseeker Mk I
ships.description: The Anvil F7C-M Super Hornet Heartseeker Mk I is a special edition of the F7C-M Super Hornet Mk I. With a unique emblem on the side of the cockpit, it is loaded with top-of-the-line components and outfitted with four imposing Behring laser cannons. The top gun turret is replaced with a S4 weapons hardpoint.[1]

ships.name: F7C-M Super Hornet Mk I
ships.description: The Anvil F7C-M Super Hornet Mk I is a civilian variant of the F7A Hornet Mk I released during its twilight in 2946 before retiring not a decade later in 2954. It was succeeded the next year in 2955 by the modernised F7C-M Super Hornet Mk II.

ships.name: F7C-M Super Hornet Mk II
ships.description: The F7C-M Super Hornet Mk II is a medium sized fighter variant of the F7C Hornet Mk II, developed by Anvil Aerospace. Anvil has enhanced their esteemed military-class superiority fighter by integrating the latest technological advancements, resulting in the F7C-M Super Hornet Mk II. This variant preserves the dual cockpit and ball turret features that contributed to the formidable reputation of its predecessor in combat scenarios.[1]

items.label: Anvil Hornet Mk II S3 Nose Turret
Anvil F7C-M Mk II Ball Turret
Anvil F7C-M Mk II Missile Rack

ships.name: F7C-R Hornet Tracker Mk I
ships.description: The Anvil F7C-R Hornet Tracker Mk I is a civilian variant of the F7A Hornet Mk I and was released in its twilight in 2946, retiring the same decade in 2954 when the F7C-R was supplanted by the modernised F7C-R Hornet Tracker Mk II.

ships.name: F7C-R Hornet Tracker Mk II
ships.description: The F7C-R Hornet Tracker Mk II represents a specialized variant of the F7C Hornet Mk II, produced by Anvil Aerospace. This spacecraft is engineered to serve as a formidable reconnaissance platform, equipped with sophisticated long-range radar systems that facilitate covert operations. As a highly capable predator, it boasts advanced scanning technologies that enhance its surveillance capabilities.[1]

ships.name: F7C-S Hornet Ghost Mk I
ships.description: The Anvil F7C-S Hornet Ghost Mk I is a civilian variant of the F7A Hornet Mk I and was released during its twilight in 2946, retiring the same decade in 2954 when the F7C-S was supplanted by the modernised F7C-S Hornet Ghost Mk II.

ships.name: F7C-S Hornet Ghost Mk II
ships.description: The F7C-S Hornet Ghost Mk II is a stealth fighter variant of the F7C Hornet Mk II, developed by Anvil Aerospace. The spacecraft is designed with dual purposes: reconnaissance and ambush. It operates beneath radar detection, utilizing a signature-reducing module that enhances its capabilities for stealth missions, whether in combat or reconnaissance scenarios.[1]

ships.name: F8C Lightning
ships.description: The Anvil Aerospace F8C Lightning is the civilian variant of the F8A Lightning space superiority fighter that is used by the United Empire of Earth Navy. It closely mimics the military specs of the original F8A, and has been used in lines of defense against Vanduul invaders. It was made available in extremely limited numbers to the civilian market by Anvil to celebrate the debut of the F8A in the UEE Navy.[1][2]

ships.name: Fortune
ships.description: The Fortune is a salvage ship developed by Musashi Industrial and Starflight Concern. It represents a significant advancement in industrial operations, offering a streamlined and efficient vessel tailored for salvage operations. Designed for individual operators, this innovative ship features a groundbreaking external cargo loader that simplifies the salvaging process for solo crews. It is the ideal choice for those seeking to enhance their operational capabilities.[1][2]

ships.name: Freelancer
ships.description: The Freelancer is a medium-sized long range cargo hauler manufactured by Musashi Industrial and Starflight Concern. The Freelancer's modularity, versatility, and firepower have made it extremely popular among owner-operators and major corporations alike. The chassis also serves as the base for several specialized variants. The Freelancer was the first ship class to be developed as part of a unique technology-sharing agreement with the Xi'an government.[1]

ships.name: Freelancer DUR
ships.description: The Freelancer DUR is the exploration variant of the Freelancer ship developed by MISC. It sacrifices some of the cargo capacity of the standard Freelancer in exchange for a more advanced scanner, an expanded fuel tank, a fuel refinery and an enhanced jump drive. This may seem like a bad call to some, but those who value discovery over profit will find it to be their ship of choice.[1][2]

ships.name: Freelancer MAX
ships.description: The Freelancer MAX is a heavy transport variant of the Freelancer developed by Musashi Industrial and Starflight Concern. Compared to a stock Freelancer, the Freelancer MAX features a greatly enlarged main hold that nearly doubles the ship's cargo capacity. This makes it ideal for moving heavy equipment, vehicles, or raw materials. Its other features and capabilities are otherwise generally identical to a base Freelancer, aside from having a modified thruster arrangement (see below).[1]

ships.name: Freelancer MIS
ships.description: The Freelancer MIS is a militarized variant of the classic Freelancer ship developed by Musashi Industrial and Starflight Concern. This version sacrifices cargo capacity to make way for an internal missile loading system. As a Freelancer variant the MIS is well suited to patrol work, and with 28 size 3 missiles the MIS can punch well above its weight class. These qualities make it a popular choice for militias and independent outfits.[1]

ships.name: Fury
ships.description: The Fury, a compact vessel manufactured by the subsidiary Mirai of Musashi Industrial and Starflight Concern, serves as an exemplary snub ship specifically designed for executing swarm formation attacks. Leveraging cutting-edge Xi'an technology and boasting unparalleled agility, this spacecraft effectively asserts its dominance over the immediate spatial or aerial environment. With its advanced capabilities, the Fury emerges as a formidable force, enabling it to effectively navigate and control local space or airspace.

ships.name: Fury LX
ships.description: The Fury LX is a snub racer variant of the Fury series of ships developed by Mirai. It boasts exceptional maneuverability and incorporates advanced Xi'an technology, making it a next-generation racing ship. The Fury LX model, in particular, prioritizes maneuvering thrusters over offensive capabilities, allowing for optimal speed and agility during races. Its state-of-the-art design and engineering make it a top contender in the competitive racing industry.[1]

ships.name: Fury MX
ships.description: The Fury MX is a snub bomber variant of the Fury series of ships developed by Mirai. It exemplifies remarkable maneuverability and integrates cutting-edge Xi’an technology. This state-of-the-art vehicle is outfitted with twenty halo-mounted missiles, enabling it to proficiently engage and eliminate targets in the challenging environment of short-range space combat. The design of the Fury MX not only enhances its agility in maneuvering through complex battle scenarios but also ensures that it can deliver precision strikes against adversaries. The incorporation of advanced technology from Xi’an further amplifies its operational capabilities, making it a formidable asset for combat.[1]

ships.name: G12
ships.description: The G12 is a touring-oriented vehicle from Origin Jumpworks. Its style, light armor, 2 SCU storage capacity and a gun turret on top make it a good all-round vehicle.[1][2][3]

ships.name: G12a
ships.description: The Origin G12a combines military might with Origin's unique approach to high-end engineering. Designed for all offensive ground-based operations, it's the ideal partner for long-range perimeter patrols, intercepting assailants, and exploring dangerous new locales.[1][2][3]

ships.name: G12r
ships.description: The G12r is the racing variant of the upcoming G12 rover manufactured by Origin. It is equipped with an EMP system but possesses no armor.[1][2][3]

ships.name: Galaxy
ships.description: The Galaxy is a modular multirole ship manufactured by Roberts Space Industries.[1] Focusing on versatility, the Galaxy is capable of equipping its crew to handle any task in the 'Verse.

ships.name: Gladiator
ships.description: The Anvil Aerospace T8C Gladiator is a civilian variant of the UEEN's T8A Gladiator. Inspired by the carrier-based dive and torpedo bombers of World War II[1] and designed as a fighter-bomber companion to Anvil's own F7 Hornet series,[2] the Gladiator is equipped to carry and launch a substantial volume of ordinance at a standoff range of kilometres in addition to featuring powerful guns for close-in defense and aggression. This civilian version is also highly modifiable, including the potential to be fit to carry cargo.[3]

ships.name: Gladius
ships.description: The P5G Gladius is a light fighter produced by Aegis. Its first design was introduced in 2579 as a replacement for the Stiletto and has been updated over the years to keep up with modern technology.[1] In military circles, the Gladius is beloved for its performance and its simplicity. A fast, light fighter with a laser-focus on dogfighting, the Gladius is an ideal interceptor or escort ship.[2]

ships.name: Gladius Dunlevy
ships.description: The Gladius Dunlevy is a special edition variant of the Gladius manufactured by Aegis Dynamics. An iconic fighter with decades of successful service to the UEE military, enhanced with military components and a unique historical livery, which honors Captain Alexandra Dunlevy and Squadron 42.[1]

ships.name: Gladius Pirate
ships.description: The Gladius Pirate is a special edition light fighter manufactured by Aegis Dynamics in which the design showcases a distinctive and striking red color scheme, paying homage to the theme of piracy. The Gladius itself represents a traditional design that has undergone various enhancements to align with contemporary technological advancements and is highly regarded for both its operational efficiency and its straightforward design. As a nimble and lightweight fighter, it excels in dogfighting scenarios, making it an exemplary choice for roles such as interception or escort ship.[1]

ships.name: Gladius Valiant
ships.description: The Gladius Valiant is a special edition variant of the Gladius manufactured by Aegis Dynamics. Developed as part of the Masters of Flight series in, collaboration with the flight simulation Arena Commander, the Valiant serves as a tribute to the esteemed pilot Condi Hillard, acknowledged for being the first Human documented to triumph over a Vanduul. This variant of the Gladius is outfitted with a tailored loadout specifically designed for dogfighting, along with a unique special edition livery that pays tribute to this legendary vessel.[1]

ships.name: Glaive (replica)
ships.description: The Esperia Glaive is a symmetrical version of the Scythe reproduced for Human usage. Generally flown by Vanduul with more combat experience, they are better armed and have two huge blades/wings as opposed to one on the standard Scythe.[1]

ships.name: Golem
ships.description: The Golem is a small entry level mining ship developed by Drake Interplanetary. It is an exemplary vessel tailored for individuals aspiring to embark on a mining career. This ship is equipped with a custom mining laser and has the ability to transport 32 SCU of precious materials in two proprietary 16 SCU Containers. True to Drake's design philosophy, the Golem is a specialized craft that emphasizes practicality over unnecessary embellishments.[1]

items.label: M det vtol ext main housing (right)
M det vtol ext main housing (left)

ships.name: Golem OX
ships.description: The Golem OX is a small cargo variant of the Golem series of ships developed by Drake Interplanetary. It is engineered to serve as an ideal cargo ship for transporting supplies and equipment, catering to pilots who prioritize practicality over embellishments. The OX increases the cargo capacity of the original Golem by a factor of two and is equipped with a tractor beam, facilitating seamless cargo transfers.[1][2]

items.label: M det vtol ext main housing (right)
M det vtol ext main housing (left)

ships.name: Guardian
ships.description: The Guardian is a small Heavy Fighter developed by Mirai. Following its success in the snub market with the highly specialized Fury, Mirai expands its ambitions by entering the heavyweight space-combat arena. The Guardian exemplifies Mirai's commitment to performance-driven ship design, amplifying this philosophy to create a formidable brawler. This vessel is engineered to confront a wide array of threats, regardless of their scale or intensity.[1][2]

items.label: Geo wing arm a (right rear top)
Geo wing arm a (left rear top)

ships.name: Guardian MX
ships.description: The Guardian MX is a variant of the Guardian heavy fighter series of ships developed by Mirai. By enhancing the Guardian fighter framework with a broader array of offensive capabilities, the MX is equipped with increased firepower and armor, making it well-suited for overpowering adversaries. Although it lacks the agility of the original Guardian, this heavy fighter still demonstrates greater maneuverability compared to numerous other vessels within its category.[1][2]

items.label: Geo wing arm a left (rear top)
Geo wing arm a right (rear top)

ships.name: Guardian QI
ships.description: The Guardian QI is an interdictor variant of the Guardian manufactured by Mirai. The Guardian exemplifies resilience and proficiency in combat scenarios, demonstrating exceptional capability even when confronting adversaries of significantly larger size. The QI enhances this effectiveness by incorporating a quantum dampener, which mitigates the advantage of faster vessels, thereby leveling the playing field against more agile opponents. This feature equips the Guardian to be well-prepared for a diverse range of missions.[1]

items.label: Geo wing arm a (right rear top)
Geo wing arm a (left rear top)

ships.name: Hammerhead
ships.description: The Aegis Hammerhead is a fast, sub-capital patrol gunship bristling with six manned quad-linked turrets. These turrets are optimized for anti-fighter and anti-ordnance work and grant the ship an unparalleled ability to put up a hailstorm of firepower that swiftly removes smaller aerospace threats. It also features a generous ordnance bay with a variety of Size 3 missiles. The Hammerhead is thus well suited for supporting larger capital ships in a fleet or to act as a general patrol vessel in lower-intensity theaters.[1]

ships.name: Hammerhead Best In Show Edition
ships.description: The Hammerhead Best In Show Edition is a limited edition corvette gunship manufactured by Aegis Dynamics. It was created especially for the 'Best In Show' event at the Intergalactic Aerospace Expo 2949 and has a unique white and purple livery. It features six manned quad-linked turrets which are designed to engage fighter craft and intercept ordnance, allowing it to effectively neutralize smaller aerial threats. It also features a spacious ordnance bay for various Size 3 missiles, making the Hammerhead well-suited to support larger capital ships or serve as a versatile patrol craft in lower-intensity operations.[1]

ships.name: Hawk
ships.description: The Anvil U9C Hawk is a light fighter designed for civilian use, with a primary focus on weaponry. It is equipped with a formidable array of both lethal and non-lethal weapons, making it a highly capable combat vessel. Additionally, the ship features a single prisoner pod located in the rear, which enables the pilot to safely and securely transport a captured target. As a result of its unique capabilities, the Anvil U9C Hawk is frequently utilized by bounty hunters and local security forces.[1]

ships.name: Herald
ships.description: The Herald is a small data running ship developed by Drake Interplanetary. It is designed to be a compact, fortified vessel engineered to securely transport information from one location to another. It is equipped with a robust central engine that facilitates rapid transit and provides the necessary power for efficient data encryption and containment. Additionally, the ship boasts sophisticated encryption software and a reinforced computer core. What sets the Herald apart from other personal spacecraft is its design, which allows for straightforward 'cleaning' procedures in the event of a potential capture.[1][2]

ships.name: Hermes
ships.description: The Hermes is a medium freighter developed by Roberts Space Industries. With its substantial capacity, rapid speed, and remarkable endurance, the Hermes transforms Medium Freight for our ever-divided universe. Consistent with its reputation, RSI provides avenues for new ventures through this hauler designed for small crews yet offering significant returns. No matter the challenge or destination, the Hermes accomplishes the task.[1][2][3]

ships.name: HoverQuad
ships.description: The HoverQuad is a hoverbike developed by Consolidated Outland. It is engineered to function as a companion ground vehicle for versatile ships like the Nomad. It boasts a cutting-edge design characterized by its streamlined angular structure. This innovative configuration not only contributes to its aesthetic appeal but also plays a crucial role in optimizing its performance. At the heart of the HoverQuad's functionality are its four advanced gravlev pads, which are strategically positioned to enhance the vehicle's maneuverability, allowing it to navigate effortlessly through a variety of terrains, from rugged landscapes to urban environments.[1]

ships.name: Hull A
ships.description: The Hull A is the only light freighter of the Hull series developed by Musashi Industrial and Starflight Concern. It features the most compact design within its series, acting as an exceptionally accessible introduction to both the Hull series and the realm of substantial cargo transport. Equipped with the same telescoping spindle mechanism found in its larger counterparts and capable of delivering an impressive 64 SCU at full capacity, the Hull A is ideally suited for adventurous solo couriers eager to explore the galaxy and enhance their professional trajectories.[1]

items.label: Crusader Mercury Star Runner Nose Turret

ships.name: Hull B
ships.description: The Hull B is the only medium freighter of the Hull series of cargo ships developed by Musashi Industrial and Starflight Concern. It represents a more durable choice frequently contrasted with other vessels of comparable cargo capacity. However, while those ships are designed for extended-range exploration and various functions, the Hull B serves solely as a cargo transport. They are commonly utilized as corporate support vessels, and it is not unusual to observe multiple ships in diverse liveries during a single journey. In contrast to the other larger vessels in the series, it possesses the ability to land on a planet while fully loaded.[1][2][3]

items.label: Geo spindle plate slider base (rear left)
Geo spindle plate slider base (rear right)

ships.name: Hull C
ships.description: The Hull C is the first heavy freighter of the Hull series developed by Musashi Industrial and Starflight Concern. It is widely regarded as the most common and versatile vessel in the galaxy. Designed to occupy the middle ground between smaller, single-person transports and the massive super freighters that dominate the market, the Hull C offers expansive modularity while retaining a degree of maneuverability. With its exceptional configurability, the Hull C is the go-to choice for freighter captains seeking a reliable and adaptable cargo transport solution. Its reputation for defensibility and adaptability has made it the standard for transport jobs across known space.[1]

ships.name: Hull D
ships.description: The Hull D is a capital sized variant of the Hull series of cargo ships, developed by Musashi Industrial and Starflight Concern. It represents the upper segment of vessel sizes, featuring an extensive ship constructed around a robust framework. It is priced reasonably, making it accessible for mid-sized organizations and enterprises. Frequently, Hull D vessels serve as flagships for commercial activities; however, their substantial size necessitates the deployment of escort fighters when operating outside secure areas.[1][2][3]

ships.name: Hull E
ships.description: The Hull E is a capital sized variant of the Hull series of cargo ships, developed by Musashi Industrial and Starflight Concern. This vessel stands as the largest specialized freighter currently on the market, typically owned by significant corporations and operated with meticulous planning. The inherent lack of maneuverability associated with such a large ship necessitates that operators exercise caution when equipping turrets and arranging for escort services. Nonetheless, its potential cargo capacity and modularity are unmatched, with no other ship offering as much space for goods storage or the ability to adapt for different roles![1][2][3]

ships.name: Hurricane
ships.description: The A4A Hurricane is a heavy fighter developed by Anvil Aerospace. The spacecraft offsets its absence of luxuries with formidable weaponry, featuring six ballistic guns that can penetrate almost any obstacle. Despite its compact design, it delivers a significant strike, and its pilots have not encountered a defensive shield they are unable to breach.[1]

ships.name: Idris-M
ships.description: The Idris-M (Military) is the original variant of the Idris series frigate developed by Aegis Dynamics and used by the UEE Military. The Idris class, named after its eponymous vessel, represents the standard line of frigates within the United Empire of Earth Navy (UEEN). These ships are larger than conventional corvettes yet smaller than destroyers and their escorts. Equipped with two hangars that can accommodate three small vessels and one snub craft, along with formidable weaponry, these units are frequently utilized in diverse active battle group configurations within the Human military. They fulfill a variety of functions, including extended patrols, reconnaissance of hazardous jump points, and interdiction missions.[1][2][3]

ships.name: Idris-P
ships.description: The Idris-P (Peacekeeper) is a variant of the Idris frigate developed by Aegis Dynamics. This vessel is specifically tailored for civilian use, having had all military-grade equipment removed. Featuring two hangars capable of housing three small vessels and one snub craft, along with enhanced cargo capacity and numerous gun turrets, it serves as an optimal choice for militia, corporate, and private entities in various different roles.[1][2][3]

ships.name: Intrepid
ships.description: The Intrepid is a small starter ship developed by Crusader Industries. Designed for those who are ready to embrace the future, this innovative ship is equipped to handle a variety of tasks and overcome diverse challenges. With its spacious cargo area and comfortable living accommodations, the Intrepid ensures that your solo adventures among the stars are both productive and enjoyable.[1]

ships.name: Ironclad
ships.description: The Drake Ironclad is a large armored cargo ship that is designed to safely transport large quantities of valuable cargo. Armed with a wide range of turrets and boasting a thick armored hull, the Ironclad is perfect for dangerous cargo runs in uncertain space.[1]

ships.name: Ironclad Assault
ships.description: The Drake Ironclad Assault is a versatile combined-arms platform which excels in transporting heavy vehicles and infantry squads, complemented by formidable auxiliary turrets for direct combat support. Furthermore, equipped with a comprehensive garage facility capable of refueling, rearming, and manufacturing new components for ground vehicles, this self-sustaining behemoth is well-equipped to overcome any obstacles or adversaries encountered on the battlefield.[1]

ships.name: Javelin
ships.description: The Aegis Javelin is a destroyer utilized by the United Empire of Earth Navy (UEEN), and the smallest ship-of-the-line serving in the UEEN.[1] Designed as a modular spacecraft, the UEEN could make major conversions to the Javelin for different roles rather than constructing individual specialized capital ships. Javelins are used as convoy escorts, fleet support, orbital fire suppression, reconnaissance in force, and fast interception missions. Surplus Javelins have been sold on the civilian market.[2] The ship features a total of five decks, each with a variety of crew positions and areas to explore and utilize.[3]

ships.name: Khartu-al
ships.description: The Khartu-al ( Xi'an: Kal'tuāl@font-face{font-family:"TemplateStyles-xiinthlea-Regular";src:local("xiinthlea"),local("xiinthlea Regular"),local("xiinthlea-Regular"),url("https://starcitizen.tools/resources/fonts/xiinthlea-Regular.woff2")format("woff2"),url("https://starcitizen.tools/resources/fonts/xiinthlea-Regular.woff")format("woff");font-weight:normal;font-style:normal}.mw-parser-output .lang-kyexiin{font-family:"TemplateStyles-xiinthlea-Regular";font-feature-settings:"liga"1,"calt"1,"dlig"1,"salt"1}(Kal'tuāl).mw-parser-output ruby{line-height:1.25}.mw-parser-output ruby>rt{font-size:0.65rem} (Proper); literally Dire fly's dance; ).mw-parser-output .lang__label{font-size:0.875rem} is an export model of the Qhire Khartu fighter developed by the Xi'an Aopoa Corporation. This vehicle is tailored for the human civilian sector, serving as a specialized scout and explorer. It features the same Xi'an maneuvering system but is modified with control surfaces suitable for human use and has a limited armament capacity.[1]

ships.name: Kraken Privateer
ships.description: The Kraken Privateer, manufactured by Drake, is a variant of the Kraken intended to serve as a mobile marketplace or outpost. 'The ship can accommodate many ships and also offer habitation for travelers that may want to stay a little longer.'[1]

ships.name: L-21 Wolf
ships.description: The L-21 Wolf is a small light Fighter developed by Kruger Intergalactic. It illustrates a combination of swiftness, grace, and an extraordinarily precise design. This vessel not only represents Kruger's inaugural independently produced ship and showcases their expertise, but it is also thoroughly outfitted for warfare, able to function autonomously or in coordination with others, armed with an impressive array of armaments.[1][2][3]

ships.name: L-22 Alpha Wolf
ships.description: The L-22 Alpha Wolf is a small light Fighter manufactured by Kruger Intergalactic. Merging speed, elegance, and an exceptionally accurate design, the Alpha Wolf serves as a demonstration for Kruger while also being fully equipped for combat, capable of hunting solo or in groups with a formidable selection of weaponry.[1][2]

ships.name: Legionnaire
ships.description: The Legionnaire is a small boarding craft manufactured by Anvil Aerospace. When the operation calls for a hostile takeover of an enemy vehicle or station, the Anvil Legionnaire is ready to report for duty. With eight drop seats, versatile docking options, and an advanced security infiltration system, even the most difficult of boarding actions is feasible with the Legionnaire in your fleet.[1]

ships.name: Liberator
ships.description: The Liberator is a vehicle transporter[1] manufactured by Anvil Aerospace specializing in "open-air" vehicle transport. With three landing pads and an expansive vehicle garage, the Liberator is capable of delivering three ships and two vehicles to the front line of any operation.

ships.name: Lynx
ships.description: The Lynx is a luxury exploration vehicle developed by Roberts Space Industries specifically as a pairing for the Constellation Phoenix.[1] The Lynx is adapted from the Ursa rover platform, allowing RSI to marry the tried and tested reliability of the existing platform with the luxury feel of the Phoenix. The Lynx loses some of the Ursa's additional armor plating and modularity for luxury upgrades.[2]

ships.name: M2 Hercules Starlifter
ships.description: The M2 Hercules Starlifter (nickname: "Mama Bird")[1] is a military protean vehicle transport that supports strategic lift and landing operations (i.e. Sword of Hope). It is developed by Crusader Industries.[2] It is the UEE's premier tactical starlifter. The ship's potent combination of capacity, maneuverability, and durability make it the obvious choice in large-scale transport, and a robust weapons package assures your cargo, and crew, gets to where they're going in one piece.[3]

ships.name: M50
ships.description: The M50 is a small, single-seat racing ship that is manufactured by Origin Jumpworks. This craft is meticulously designed to cater to those seeking to traverse distances swiftly while exuding elegance and sophistication. With its sleek lines and aerodynamic profile, the M50 is not just a means of transportation; it is a statement of style and engineering prowess. It is often referred to as a power plant with a cockpit attached, it is equipped with supercharged engines that are designed to prioritize speed over a substantial weapons payload.[1][2]

ships.name: MDC
ships.description: The MDC (Mobile Defense Center) is a ground combat vehicle developed by Greycat Industrial. Constructed on a strong and durable industrial framework of the MTC, the MDC is engineered to safeguard your interests, ranging from distant mining locations to patrolling high-value processing facilities, all made possible by its integrated point-defense turret.[1][2][3]

ships.name: MOLE
ships.description: The MOLE (Multi Operator Laser Extractor) is a medium-sized multi-crew mining ship developed by Argo Astronautics. Equipped with three independent mining turrets and extensive crew facilities, this vessel is ideally suited for effective multi-crew mining operations. Furthermore, a substantial collection of twenty four 12SCU mineral pods guarantees that no valuable resources will be overlooked. The MOLE embodies the principle that "Many hands make light work".[1]

ships.name: MOLE Carbon
ships.description: The MOLE Carbon is a distinctive variant of the MOLE (Multi Operator Laser Extractor) medium-sized mining vessel, manufactured by Argo Astronautics. This special edition was specifically developed to commemorate the introduction of the MOLE and features a distinctive dark grey colored exterior. The ship is equipped with three operational mining turrets and extensive crew amenities, making it ideally suited for effective multi-crew mining operations. Furthermore, it boasts a substantial collection of twenty-four mineral pods, guaranteeing that no valuable resources will be overlooked. The MOLE embodies the principle that collaborative efforts lead to increased efficiency.[1][2]

ships.name: MOLE Talus
ships.description: The MOLE Talus is a distinctive variant of the MOLE (Multi Operator Laser Extractor) medium-sized mining vessel, manufactured by Argo Astronautics. This special edition was specifically developed to commemorate the introduction of the MOLE and features a distinctive light grey colored exterior. The ship is equipped with three operational mining turrets and extensive crew amenities, making it ideally suited for effective multi-crew mining operations. Furthermore, it boasts a substantial collection of twenty-four mineral pods, guaranteeing that no valuable resources will be overlooked. The MOLE embodies the principle that collaborative efforts lead to increased efficiency.[1][2]

ships.name: MOTH
ships.description: The MOTH (Multi Operator Targeted Harvester) is a medium salvager developed by Argo Astronautics. Tailored specifically for operators aiming to elevate their small-scale recovery efforts, the MOTH is purposefully constructed to connect the divide between light salvage operations and comprehensive industrial projects. It is equipped with the necessary tools to justify its value, offers ample storage for extended tasks, and possesses the drive to enhance your team's performance to a higher level.[1][2]

ships.name: MPUV Cargo
ships.description: The MPUV Cargo (Multi Purpose Utility Vehicle Cargo) also known as MPUV-1C is a light transport snub ship developed by Argo Astronautics and is designed to transfer small amounts of freight as well a single passenger over a short distance. Functioning as a common intergalactic Stevedore , a significant number of MPUV Cargos facilitate the loading and unloading of cargo onto ships that are unable to land on planetary surfaces or in dry-docks, including vessels like the Hull D and the Orion. Some captains of hauler ships opt to possess and manage their own MPUV, while others prefer to engage the services of local MPUV owners available at numerous bustling ports. Numerous MPUV Cargos are employed to facilitate ship-to-ship transfers between capital vessels, including the Javelin and Idris.[1][2][3]

ships.name: MPUV Personnel
ships.description: The MPUV Personnel (Multi-Purpose Utility Vehicle Personnel) also known as MPUV-1P is a personal transport snub ship developed by Argo Astronautics and is primarily designed for transporting up to eight individuals from one location to another over a short range. The United Empire of Earth Navy relies heavily on MPUV Personnel ships, and new recruits often remember the daunting experience of being transported by this vessel to their initial space mission. They are usually employed to facilitate ship-to-ship transfers between capital vessels, including the Javelin and Idris. In civilian applications, MPUV Personnel ships are modified for a variety of purposes, ranging from conventional taxi services to improvised combat dropships.[1][2][3]

ships.name: MPUV Tractor
ships.description: The MPUV Tractor (Multi Purpose Utility Vehicle Tractor) also known as MPUV-1T is a light transport snub ship developed by Argo Astronautics and is designed to transfer freight over a short distance. Given its role, it does not have a slot for a quantum drive. Its chassis is equipped with two tractor beams to facilitate the handling of cargo, showcasing the distinctive Argo construction excellence, and a flexible configuration that can accommodate diverse loading tasks. Setting benchmarks in terms of rapidity and effectiveness, it is challenging to locate a freight business of high caliber that does not rely on a fleet of MPUVs for its operations.[1][2]

ships.name: MTC
ships.description: The MTC (Mobile Tactical Center) is a ground vehicle developed by Greycat Industrial. As a specialist in industrial operations, Greycat recognizes that ensuring security at job sites is crucial for the success of any business endeavor. Furthermore, to maximize effectiveness, it is essential to deploy security personnel and their equipment to the locations where they are most needed. This is why the MTC represents a significant enhancement for your upcoming project. Its agility allows it to navigate challenging terrains typical of remote operations, and it is sufficiently equipped with suit lockers, weapon racks, and ample storage to address a variety of challenges. The MTC could be instrumental in transforming a potential issue into a lucrative opportunity.[1][2]

ships.name: Mantis
ships.description: The Mantis is a single-seat interdiction vessel developed by Roberts Space Industries. It possesses the ability to extract targets from quantum travel through a mechanism known as "Quantum Enforcement." With minimal armament and armor, the Mantis is intended to operate alongside more heavily armed law enforcement agencies or pirates to engage with the apprehended vessels. [1][2]

ships.name: Merchantman
ships.description: The Banu Merchantman is a trading ship capable of landing or docking and then inviting locals in to view what its cargo holds have to offer. The ship design, perfected by generations of Banu development, is intended to offer a combination of high speed and durability. While the Merchantman is less configurable than a Caterpillar or a Starfarer, there's still plenty of room to take the ship in different directions.[1]

ships.name: Mercury Star Runner
ships.description: The Mercury Star Runner is a medium sized courier ship developed by Crusader Industries. For those requiring swift and secure delivery, the Mercury fulfills all the criteria of a reliable courier ship and more. Constructed using the engineering and design standards that have established Crusader Industries as the preferred producer for interstellar transport of any magnitude, the Mercury Star Runner enables you to maintain a lead over deadlines, challenges, and rivals.[1]

items.label: Crusader Mercury Star Runner Nose Turret

ships.name: Meteor
ships.description: The Meteor is a medium fighter variant of the Mantis series of ships, developed by Roberts Space Industries. Equipped with formidable weaponry, the Meteor presents a daunting challenge for adversaries. Drawing influence from the legendary Mantis, this medium fighter opts out of quantum enforcement, incorporating instead two custom S5 ballistic cannons and four single-load S4 missile racks, thereby establishing the Meteor as a powerful entity capable of illuminating the sky akin to a shooting star.[1][2]

items.label: Dbr body spine side fin left (top)
Dbr body spine side fin right (top)

ships.name: Mule
ships.description: The Mule is an single-seat ground cargo hauler developed by Drake Interplanetary. The six-wheeled Drake Mule is a dependable workhouse for all sorts of loading and hauling jobs. Whether transporting cargo over rough terrain or ferrying it onto larger vessels and cargo decks, the Mule sees even the toughest contracts through to the end.[1]

ships.name: Mustang Alpha
ships.description: The Consolidated Outland Mustang Alpha is a sleek, stylish spacecraft that uses ultralight alloys to push power ratios to the limits, albeit sometimes unsafely as it is inspired by Consolidated Outland CEO Silas Koerner's cutting edge vision. And now, with the optional Cargo Carrier, you can have the Alpha's advantages without sacrificing carrying capacity.[1]

ships.name: Mustang Alpha Vindicator
ships.description: The limited edition Mustang Alpha 'Vindicator' is a single-seat civilian variant of the Mustang series spacecraft developed by Consolidated Outland. This model celebrates the CitizenCon 2948 event and embodies the adventurous spirit that defines the original vision of Silas Koerner, as well as the unwavering determination of the UEE. It features an underside cargo carrier and is designed to serve as a formidable competitor to the RSI Aurora series of spacecraft.[1]

ships.name: Mustang Beta
ships.description: The Mustang Beta is a Pathfinder variant of the Mustang series spacecraft developed by Consolidated Outland. Designed specifically for extended travels tailored to cost-conscious explorers, the Beta can effortlessly reach across the 'Verse, thanks to its larger fuel tanks, while its distinctive living quarters ensure that the voyage feels almost as comfortable as being at home.[1][2]

ships.name: Mustang Delta
ships.description: The Mustang Delta is the militia variant of Mustang spacecraft developed by Consolidated Outland. Although it may not match the capabilities of certain military-specific vessels, the Delta enhances the Mustang's robust hull design by incorporating Consolidated Outland's Cavalry Class Mass Reduction Armor, resulting in a diminished cross-sectional signature that levels the competitive landscape.[1][2]

ships.name: Mustang Gamma
ships.description: The Mustang Gamma is a racing variant of the Mustang series spacecraft developed by Consolidated Outland. As the inaugural racing variant of the Mustang, engineers have successfully reduced the total weight of the initial model, while simultaneously improving the standard engines and incorporating an additional main thruster This redesign results in improved maneuverability compared to the standard Mustang, albeit at the cost of a reduction in missile and weaponry capacity.[1][2]

ships.name: Mustang Omega
ships.description: The Mustang Omega is a limited edition racing variant of the Mustang Series spacecraft developed by Consolidated Outland who collaborated with the custom tuning firm Accelerated Mass Design (AMD). The Omega features an extra main thruster and a ramped up fuel intake for faster recycling of the ship’s already impressive boost system. To cap off the collaboration, AMD enlisted resident underground artist Sektor8 to design the dynamic paint job.[1][2]

ships.name: Nautilus
ships.description: The Aegis Nautilus is a large military minelayer ship, with fully-integrated mine-deployment, sweeping and disarming capabilities. Originally introduced in 2549, the ship has had centuries of refinement until reintroduced in 2949 for its 400th anniversary.[1]

ships.name: Nomad
ships.description: The Nomad is a small multi-purpose freighter developed by Consolidated Outland. It incorporates an array of innovative features within a sleek and compact design, featuring a spacious flatbed cargo area, advanced hover technology, and a comfortable living space, establishing the Nomad as a benchmark of self-sufficiency and creativity.[1][2]

ships.name: Nova
ships.description: The Tumbril Nova is a fully comprehensive battle tank, reimagined for the modern age. This heavy tank offers a devastating combination of weaponry to eliminate threats on the ground and in the air.[1] Initially designed during the Second Tevarin War, the Nova battle tank has been a staple of ground warfare operations ever since.[1]

ships.name: Nox
ships.description: The Nox is a small open-canopy racing vehicle developed by the Xi'an Aopoa corporation. It is designed to adeptly traverse various planetary terrains and outer space. This groundbreaking model has been reengineered for Human pilots, representing a significant advancement in competitive racing. Its innovative design enables high-speed performance while addressing the unique challenges of both terrestrial and extraterrestrial environments, establishing a new benchmark in racing technology.[1][2]

ships.name: Nox Kue
ships.description: The Nox Kue is a limited edition open-canopy racer produced by Aopoa that capable of flying along planet surfaces or through deep space. It is named after the Xi'an term for 'thrust' ( Xi'an: Kue3@font-face{font-family:"TemplateStyles-xiinthlea-Regular";src:local("xiinthlea"),local("xiinthlea Regular"),local("xiinthlea-Regular"),url("https://starcitizen.tools/resources/fonts/xiinthlea-Regular.woff2")format("woff2"),url("https://starcitizen.tools/resources/fonts/xiinthlea-Regular.woff")format("woff");font-weight:normal;font-style:normal}.mw-parser-output .lang-kyexiin{font-family:"TemplateStyles-xiinthlea-Regular";font-feature-settings:"liga"1,"calt"1,"dlig"1,"salt"1}(Kue”).mw-parser-output ruby{line-height:1.25}.mw-parser-output ruby>rt{font-size:0.65rem} (Proper); ).mw-parser-output .lang__label{font-size:0.875rem}. The vehicle showcases an exquisite brushed-silver finish and was designed to commemorate the initial sale of the first Nox intended for Human riders.[1]

ships.name: Odyssey
ships.description: The Odyssey by MISC is a Capital-sized, highly advanced multi-crew explorer ship that is optimized for long-duration expeditions. This robust vessel boasts an on-board refinery and mining gear along with a single small hangar, a medical bay, and accommodations for a crew of 6.[1]

ships.name: Orion
ships.description: The Orion is a capital mining ship manufactured by Roberts Space Industries (RSI). It is a mobile mining platform that has extensive storage for resources, drone docks, and an on-board refinery, which allows the ship to be operated alone for large-scale mining operations without the need of support vessels. It is currently the largest civilian ship sold by RSI, and the largest mining ship in Star Citizen.[1][2]

ships.name: P-52 Merlin
ships.description: The P-52 Merlin is a snub fighter created by Kruger Intergalactic, initially intended to complement the RSI Constellation series of ships. Equipped with a central gatling cannon and a Lightning Power engine, the Merlin is characterized by its speed and agility, delivering an impressive performance in combat. It is particularly well-suited for local reconnaissance, rapid engagements, and even racing activities.[1]

ships.name: P-72 Archimedes
ships.description: The P-72 Archimedes is a luxury snub racer developed by Kruger Intergalactic. It caters to individuals seeking a more nimble spacecraft, ideal for traversing the cosmos. Whether the objective is enhanced security, system exploration, or the sheer pleasure of flight, the Archimedes serves as an exemplary companion snub craft. With its additional intake and lightweight hull, it offers remarkable handling and acceleration, all encapsulated in an elegant design that makes it a desirable addition to any journey.[1]

ships.name: P-72 Archimedes Emerald
ships.description: The P-72 Archimedes Emerald is a distinctive variant of the P-72 Archimedes luxury snub racer manufacture by Kruger Intergalactic. This special edition was specifically designed to commemorate the widely celebrated Stella Fortuna holiday and features a unique grey. green and orange color scheme. The P-72 caters to individuals seeking a more nimble spacecraft, ideal for traversing the cosmos. Whether the objective is enhanced security, system exploration, or the sheer pleasure of flight, the Archimedes serves as an exemplary companion snub craft. With its additional intake and lightweight hull, it offers remarkable handling and acceleration, all encapsulated in an elegant design that makes it a desirable addition to any journey.[1]

ships.name: PTV
ships.description: The PTV (Personal Transport Vehicle, commonly known as Greycat Buggy) is a small ground vehicle developed by Greycat Industrial. PTVs are intended for travel and transport of two personnel commonly used in spacecraft hangars and on flight lines to deliver crews to spacecraft stored outside comfortable walking distance.[1]

ships.name: Paladin
ships.description: The Paladin is a medium sized gunship, developed by Anvil Aerospace. Its primary purpose is to serve at the forefront of Humanity's defense, equipping itself with advanced weaponry to safeguard the UEE from both external and internal threats. Upholding a longstanding tradition, the Paladin exhibits no mercy, systematically neutralizing dangers with an unyielding barrage of fire. Featuring an enhanced arsenal of weapons, including an increased number of guns and missiles, the Paladin delivers the powerful performance anticipated from a next-generation gunship, fully prepared for the evolving landscape of warfare.[1][2][3]

ships.name: Perseus
ships.description: The Perseus is a large patrol gunship manufactured by Roberts Space Industries. Initially created in the late 2520s for the UPE Navy, it underwent a redesign in 2950 to become a civilian model, incorporating contemporary components and focusing on enhanced utility. Originally designed for blockade operations and confrontations with sub-capital vessels, the Perseus is equipped with robust turrets and a torpedo launch bay.[1][2][3]

ships.name: Pioneer
ships.description: The Consolidated Outland Pioneer is an upcoming self-contained mobile construction yard capable of creating planetary modular structures, allowing colonists to customize their new homes on the frontier.[1] Utilizing an on-board reserve of resources, the Pioneer can construct and deploy modular outposts as well as continue to build additional modules to turn your outpost into a settlement.[2][3]

ships.name: Polaris
ships.description: The Polaris is a corvette-class capital ship developed by Roberts Space Industries (RSI) for the United Empire of Earth Navy (UEEN). Designed for use as both a naval patrol ship and to serve as the flagship of civilian militia operations, the Polaris has the capacity to perform search & rescue operations, strike missions and general security patrols. While intended for combat situations, the Polaris can also operate as a command center while providing refuel and repair capabilities from its single small fighter bay. [1][2][3]
